Document:
# Currency
[textlink="View Full Wiki Page" link="https://ss14.misfitsystems.net/wiki/index.php/Currency"/]
◈ Currency ◈ Wasteland Economy ◈
The Wasteland runs on trade, barter, and cold hard currency. Multiple forms of money circulate in the Wendover region - each backed by different powers and carrying different weight depending on who you are dealing with.
## Currency Types
[bold]Currency[/bold] [bold]Description[/bold] [bold]Value[/bold] [bold]Denominations[/bold] Bottlecaps The universal wasteland currency. Accepted everywhere, trusted by everyone. The standard unit of exchange. 1 cap = 1 unit 1, 10, 50, 100, 500 [bold]NCR Dollars[/bold] Paper money issued by the New California Republic. Backed by the Republic's gold reserves - or what remains of them. Worth less than caps due to inflation and limited acceptance outside NCR territory. [bold]0.5[/bold] per dollar 10, 50, 100, 500 [bold]Legion Aureus[/bold] Gold coins minted by Caesar's Legion. Solid gold, universally recognized as valuable regardless of political opinion. One Aureus equals 10 Denarii. 10× Denarius Individual coins [bold]Legion Denarius[/bold] Silver coins of the Legion. The base denomination of Legion currency. Widely accepted as a medium of exchange even by factions hostile to the Legion - silver is silver. Base unit Individual coins [bold]Pre-War Money[/bold] Old-world paper currency. Worthless as legal tender but collected as a curiosity, used as kindling, or traded to those who value pre-war artifacts. Cosmetic / 1 per unit 10, 50, 100, 500
## Wallets
[bold]Wallets[/bold] (belt or ID slot) can hold multiple currency types and keep your money organized. All items tagged as [bold]Currency[/bold] are eligible for wallet storage.
## Economic Notes
Bottlecaps are the [bold]default medium of exchange[/bold] throughout the Wasteland
NCR Dollars trade at [bold]half value[/bold] - two dollars for one cap's worth of goods
Legion currency is accepted widely due to its [bold]metal content[/bold] (gold and silver), regardless of political alignment
Pre-War Money has [bold]no standardized trade value[/bold] - its worth depends entirely on who wants it
